title: tanarius oblongifolius
intro: Tanarius oblongifolius, also known as Tanacetum parthenium or Feverfew, is a perennial plant native to Asia Minor and the Balkans, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic compounds. The health benefits of Tanarius oblongifolius include reducing fever, relieving headaches and migraines, and alleviating menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Tanarius oblongifolius include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
